name: Simulacra
description: Economic policy simulation with 25 AI agents in a pixel-art city — 1st Place at YHacks '26 K2 Think V2 Track
content:
# Simulacra (1st Place at YHacks - K2 Think V2 Track)

Economic policy simulation where 25 AI agents react to real policy proposals in a pixel-art city.

## How it works
- Paste ~500 words of economic policy (e.g. "Raise minimum wage to $20/hr")
- 25 NPCs spawn: workers, business owners, politicians, students, retirees, activists, farmers, shopkeepers, drivers
- 15 simulation rounds run across 3 phases — each NPC perceives, retrieves memories, reflects, plans, and acts
- Social influence spreads via proximity-based opinion dynamics (Deffuant bounded confidence, Baumann polarization)
- Live dashboard tracks price index, unemployment, and social unrest with an interactive force-layout social graph

## Features
- Click any NPC to inspect mood, income, political leaning, internal thoughts, and current plan
- Bankruptcy markers, floating money effects, and emotion indicators animate in real time
- WebSocket streaming for live simulation updates
- Multi-agent orchestration with LangGraph's async execution
